For Individual Entrepreneurs: in addition to the above documents it is necessary to provide proof of the activity of the applicant, such as:
an extract from the USRPE (Unified State Register of Private Entrepreneurs) or a certificate for individual entrepreneurial activity,
AND
2NDFL or 3NDFL forms,
AND
bank account(-s) statement(-s) with funds movement for the last 3 months.
- MEMBERS OF OFFICIAL DELEGATIONS
For members of official delegations, who on the basis of an official invitation, participate in meetings, counsels, negotiations or exchange programs, as well as in events held by intergovernmental organizations, the following should be attached:
an official letter from the Croatian or Russian competent authority, confirming that the applicant is a member of the delegation of the authority or institution, who is sent to Croatia to participate in these events attaching an official invitation.
The official invitation should contain the following information:
- about the invited person – name and surname, date of birth, gender, nationality, identity document number, duration and purpose of travel, and number of entries;
- about the inviting party - the full name and address, as well as the name, surname and position of the person who signed the official invitation;
- OIB (personal identification number) – if it is a legal entity, trading company, institution or their branch established in Croatia;
- TIN (taxpayer identification number - INN) – if it is a legal entity, trading company, institution or their branch based in the Russian Federation.

- DRIVERS (INTERNATIONAL FREIGHT AND PASSENGER TRANSPORT)
Drivers of transport providing international transport of goods and passengers services between the Russian Federation and Member States using transport registered in the Member States or in the Russian Federation (ASMAP or RAS), should submit a written request from the national association / trade union.
The written request should contain information:
- about the invited person – name and surname, date of birth, gender, nationality, identity document number, duration and purpose of travel, and number of entries;
- about the inviting party - the full name and address, as well as the name, surname and position of the person who signed the official invitation;
- OIB (personal identification number) – if it is a legal entity, trading company, institution or their branch established in Croatia;
- TIN (taxpayer identification number - INN) – if it is a legal entity, trading company, institution or their branch based in the Russian Federation.
- CREWS OF RAILWAY TRAINS, REFRIGERATORS AND LOCOMOTIVES
Crews of railway trains, refrigerators and locomotives should submit a written request from the relevant railway trading company from the Russian Federation or Member States, indicating the purpose, duration and frequency of travel.
The written request should contain information:
- about the invited person – name and surname, date of birth, gender, nationality, identity document number, duration and purpose of travel, and number of entries;
- about the inviting party - the full name and address, as well as the name, surname and position of the person who signed the official invitation;
- OIB (personal identification number) – if it is a legal entity, trading company, institution or their branch established in Croatia;
- TIN (taxpayer identification number - INN) – if it is a legal entity, trading company, institution or their branch based in the Russian Federation.
- JOURNALISTS/INDEPENDENT JOURNALISTS (up to three months)
Journalists should submit:
- a certificate from the Union of Journalists confirming that the applicant is a qualified journalist,
AND
- employer's certificate confirming that the purpose of travel is journalistic activity.
Independent/Freelance journalists should submit:
- confirmation of performing journalistic activities (for example: a correspondent certificate or contract of employment, etc.)
AND
- confirmation of economic activity/availability of financial funds:
- an extract from the USRPE or a certificate for individual entrepreneurial activity,
AND
- 2NDFL or 3NDFL forms,
AND
- bank account(-s) statement(-s) with funds movement for the last 3 months.
- SCIENTISTS RECEIVING GRANTS FROM PUBLIC FOUNDATIONS OR INVITED BY UNIVERSITIES OR OTHER PUBLIC EDUCATIONAL AND RESEARCH ORGANIZATIONS (up to three months)
- HOLIDAYS JOB AND EXCHANGE PROGRAMS (up to three months)
- SEAFARERS IN TRANSIT
A maritime agency notified of the arrival of a crew member for embarkation or disembarkation in Croatia, should provide the seafarer with an invitation letter (may be provided in a copy). In accordance with article 18, paragraph 2 of the Visa Code (HH 7/2013 , NN 109/2021) the invitation letter should contain:
- information about the seafarer – name and surname, date of birth, nationality, travel document number, place of issue and validity of the travel document;
- purpose of arrival;
- the period during which the foreign citizen (seafarer) will stay in Croatia;
- information about accommodation of a foreign citizen (if necessary);
- information about who bears the expenses for the stay of a foreign citizen in Croatia;
- name and surname, position and place of work, address and telephone number of a contact person from a maritime agency who can provide relevant information about a foreign citizen;
- confirmation of the seafarer's professional status (sailor's passport, employment contract, etc.).
Seafarers are exempted from the requirement to provide evidence of valid medical insurance.

## Photo Specifications
- When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 2 photographs as part of completing and submitting your visa application.
- If you haven’t opportunity to make photo in advance, you can make photo in our VAC.

## Photo Requirements

## General requirements
- Photo for each visa applicant must be an un mounted, full face photo as described below
-  Photo must be taken within the last 6 months, for small children within the last 3 months..
Photo size: Photo submitted must measure as below mentioned 
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photo must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly, with closed mouth, without smile.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photo must be in color, made on matte or glossy paper and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                It is preferable that ears be exposed.
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photo depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Attention: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photos presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete
